title: My Rest Is Not Laziness. It Is Necessary.
subtitle: An affirmation for taking rest without the guilt that usually accompanies it
excerpt: A short affirmation for rest taken without apology. For the woman who cannot sit down without narrating the list she should be doing instead.

content.text: A short affirmation for rest taken without the commentary. Rest is one of the things that women are most likely to report guilt about: the sense of doing nothing when there is always more to do. The guilt keeps rest from being restorative, because the body stops but the internal narrator keeps going. This affirmation is for interrupting that narrator before rest begins.
Sit down deliberately. Say it once, out loud if possible:
My rest is not laziness. It is necessary. I am doing something important right now.

The first sentence names the misclassification and corrects it. The second makes rest a requirement, not an indulgence. The third insists on the present action: resting is something you are doing, not something you are failing to do.
